svg.text: Closed-loop rollout: a spread model predicts an unseen condition from its first pool
Fit on other runs, the model rolls the whole trajectory forward and never reads the held-out run's later candidates,
spread, agreement, or value. Validation is leave-one-condition-out: the entire intervention / judge regime is held
out. Lower endpoint MAE (mean absolute error of the predicted final value vs the true final value, 0-1 scale) is
Does the closed loop predict an unseen condition?
Per regime: closed-loop endpoint error vs the no-change baseline (value never moves from round 1). The model sees only
the held-out run's first pool, then rolls forward; on selection-driven runs it gets 28/31 large-move directions right.
closed loop — rolls the trajectory forward from round 1
no-change — predicts the round-1 value forever (baseline)
endpoint MAE (mean absolute error vs true final value) — lower is better
out of scope: the judge changes mid-run — a state the round-1 model cannot see (the only regime where closed-loop loses)
Definition vs dynamics: which spread scale transports?
Selection-driven endpoint MAE, whole condition held out (leave-one-condition-out), for the nine spread definitions that
exist on both score axes (risk option 0/1 and continuous self-description). Ordered best (top) to worst (bottom).
fraction of prompts with any difference
selection-driven endpoint MAE — lower is better
Range beats mean SD by 0.007 endpoint MAE under geometry (paired bootstrap 95% CI 0.003–0.011);
under frozen the gap is only 0.002 and its interval crosses zero. Total pooled SD and median within-prompt SD (bottom two) are clearly worse.
“Fraction of prompts with any difference” wins under leave-one-RUN-out but reverses here (0.150) — it does not transport, so it is not crowned.
Mean-SD geometry predicts the risk spread trajectory better than freezing it (spread MAE 0.080 vs 0.111) yet predicts value
endpoints worse (0.139 vs 0.127) — feeding predicted spread back does not help the endpoint. The best simple endpoint model is
frozen rankable support: MAE 0.125 vs 0.431 no-change, 31/31 large-move directions right. Binary entropy (risk-0/1 runs only) scores
0.128 but is not comparable across both axes, so it is excluded from this ranking.
